name: Saola

visual_attributes.morphology: quadrupedal with a robust body and long, pointed horns
distinctive_features: nearly parallel long horns present on both sexes
chocolate brown coat with white patches on face, throat, and neck
black dorsal stripe from shoulders to tail
visual_attributes.texture: soft and thin hair, woolly on the insides of forelegs and belly
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 84 cm at the shoulder, 150 cm in body length
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: elevated back, slightly taller than shoulder height

domain_specific_visual.COAT_PATTERN: chocolate brown with white patches and black dorsal stripe

domain_specific_functional.ECOLOGICAL_ROLE: part of the forest ecosystem in the Annamite Range

relationships.description: The saola is the earliest diverging member of the tribe Bovini, closely related to buffalo and cattle.
The saola is native to the Annamite Range in Vietnam and Laos.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: restricted to high-altitude wet evergreen forests in Vietnam and Laos
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: referred to as 'Asian unicorn' due to rarity and gentle nature

contrastive_constraints: NOT without distinctive long, parallel horns
NOT associated with Western unicorn myths
